channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rubio says Trump’s interest in Greenland and Panama Canal is legit and driven by China concerns
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says President Donald Trump’s desire to acquire Greenland and retake control of the Panama Canal is driven by legitimate national security interests stemming from growing concerns about Chinese activity in the Arctic and in Latin America.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexican border cities can expect higher unemployment, bankruptcies with Trump tariffs, Baja business leaders say
item.description: According to Vega Álvarez, consumers on both sides of the border will be the ones who will pay for the tariffs as "things will have to be overpriced.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee GOP passes school voucher expansion bill with backing from Trump
item.description: Tennessee lawmakers have approved legislation drastically expanding the number of families who can use taxpayer money on private schools regardless of income.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mattise And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's FBI chief pick, Kash Patel, insists he has no 'enemies list' and won't seek retribution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, insisted to deeply skeptical Democrats on Thursday that he did not have an “enemies list" and that the bureau under his leadership would not seek retribution against the president's adversaries or launch investigations for
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kansas lawmakers vow to find answers after Wichita plane crash in DC
item.description: House and Senate lawmakers from Kansas released a bipartisan statement on the tragic American Airlines crash Wednesday night in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kash Patel enrages Adam Schiff in Clintonian battle over the word 'we' and a January 6 song
item.description: Kash Patel battled Sen. Adam Schiff, D-Calif., during his FBI directorial confirmation hearing, particularly in regard to the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump backed 2024 Republican Senate nominee in Michigan moves closer to making another run in 2026
item.description: Former Rep. Mike Rogers of Michigan says he's considering a second straight Republican run for U.S. Senate days after Democratic Sen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump consoles crash victims then dives into politics with attack on diversity initiatives
item.description: At a news conference on Thursday, President Donald Trump veered into politics to speculate that Democrats and diversity initiatives could be to blame for the deaths of 67 people in the incident.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Vance suggests some reporters covering the Trump White House are biased
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance is suggesting that some reporters covering the Trump White House are biased — perhaps even Democratic “propagandists” — and the new administration will keep a close eye on those asking questions at press briefings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr., Patel hearings set to consume Capitol Hill
item.description: Hearing-mania is set to consume Capitol Hill on Thursday as a trio of President Trump's top allies appear for high-stakes confirmation showdowns with senators that could make or break their chances of getting across the finish line. Tulsi Gabbard, Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trans community fears Trump's actions will upend legal precedent on prison protections
item.description: One of President Trump's first executive orders impacts transgender inmates incarcerated in federal facilities. It's just one in a series of actions signed by Trump that target the trans community.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Trump order cracks down on antisemitism and could deport foreign student protesters
item.description: The executive order directs government agencies to use all available tools to prosecute or remove perpetrators of antisemitic harassment and violence, especially on college campuses.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis signals veto of TRUMP Act, criticizing lawmakers' immigration bill
item.description: After a late night for lawmakers in Tallahassee, Governor Ron DeSantis says his veto pen is ready to reject the TRUMP Act.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Idaho House calls on Supreme Court to undo same-sex marriage ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court does not overturn its previous rulings absent a new case presenting a legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's Nevada voters united on top issues while Harris backers couldn't, NVPOP poll shows
item.description: A new poll that examines the driving forces behind Nevada voters' decisions in the 2024 election shows that President Donald Trump's support came from people who were focused on two primary issues: the economy and immigration.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: President Trump orders federal funding freeze: What it means for NYers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s administration has issued a pause on all federal grants and loans, according to a memo from Matthew Vaeth, acting director of the Office of Management and Budget. The memo was sent on Monday but the freeze goes into effect Tuesday at 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Florida Republicans end DeSantis special session before calling their own
item.description: Lawmakers in Tallahassee on Monday abruptly ended a special session called by Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis to address illegal immigration and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada state senator takes on gender, sports issue at UNLV, UNR, community colleges
item.description: Republican state Senator Carrie Buck is sponsoring legislation that would require Nevada colleges and universities to restrict participation in athletics based on the athlete's gender at birth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
